items.title: Senate Crypto Bill Faces Over 100 Amendments Ahead of Thursday Markup
items.desc: Bitcoin Magazine Senate Crypto Bill Faces Over 100 Amendments Ahead of Thursday Markup The Senate crypto bill faces a high-stakes markup this Thursday as lawmakers debate 100+ amendments that could determine the future of U.S. digital asset regulation. This post Senate Crypto Bill Faces Over 100 Amendments Ahead of Thursday Markup first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.

items.title: Blockaid Launches Real-Time Compliance Suite as Institutions Deepen Crypto Exposure
items.desc: Bitcoin Magazine Blockaid Launches Real-Time Compliance Suite as Institutions Deepen Crypto Exposure Blockaid launched Risk Exposure, a real-time compliance suite bringing programmable AML controls, transaction screening, and DeFi monitoring to institutional onchain finance as banks deepen crypto exposure. This post Blockaid Launches Real-Time Compliance Suite as Institutions Deepen Crypto Exposure first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.

items.title: $11.77 Trillion Brokerage Giant Charles Schwab Launches Spot Bitcoin Trading Platform
items.desc: Bitcoin Magazine $11.77 Trillion Brokerage Giant Charles Schwab Launches Spot Bitcoin Trading Platform Charles Schwab is bringing Bitcoin trading to Main Street: the $11.77 trillion brokerage giant has launched spot BTC trading for U.S. retail clients, deepening Wall Street’s accelerating embrace of digital assets. This post $11.77 Trillion Brokerage Giant Charles Schwab Launches Spot Bitcoin Trading Platform first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.

items.title: Bitcoin price history suggests 77% odds of new all-time high within a year
items.desc: Bitcoin reduces its drop from all-time highs to 35% in a move that sparked new BTC price all-time highs "within a year" on seven occasions in the past.

items.title: Vietnam eyes Q3 launch for regulated crypto asset market: Report

items.desc: Jane Street cut Bitcoin ETF holdings sharply in Q1 2026, including IBIT and FBTC, while adding at least $82 million in Ether ETF exposure.

items.desc: The Nasdaq-listed firm said the net loss was mainly driven by $92.3 million of unrealized losses on digital assets.

items.desc: BTC is fighting a technical battle as it trades just below two closely watched long-term trend indicators: the 200-day Simple Moving Average and the 200-day Exponential Moving Average.

items.title: EToro reiterates commitment to crypto despite falling activity in Q1
items.desc: CEO Yoni Assia says he’s bullish on crypto and expects to see it back to all-time highs this year even as crypto derivatives trading on eToro dropped.

items.title: EToro profits rise as commodities boom offsets crypto trading slump
items.desc: EToro posted its strongest quarter as a public company, with net income up 37% to $82 million, but crypto trade volumes fell 32% in April.

items.title: Korean won stablecoin KRWQ expands to Solana following March EDX Markets listing
items.desc: Korean won-denominated stablecoin KRWQ is expanding to Solana to support Korean won liquidity onchain.

items.desc: The stablecoin is designed to be issued on Ethereum and Layer 1 public blockchain Japan Open Chain, which local enterprises operate.

items.title: Legend becomes latest DeFi app to throw in towel
items.desc: “The product that wins isn’t the one that explains crypto better, it’s the one that hides it completely,” said CEO Jayson Hobby.

items.title: XRP tops bitcoin, ether volumes on major South Korean exchanges
items.desc: XRP/KRW was the most traded pair on Upbit and second on Bithumb, a familiar Korean market signal that has often preceded sharper moves in the token.

items.title: Hyperliquid ETF attracts $1.2M inflows in 'very solid' US debut
items.desc: 21Shares’ Hyperliquid ETF debuted in the US to a “very solid day” of trading, despite volumes being below comparatively buzzy crypto ETF debuts.

items.title: Charles Schwab launches spot BTC, ETH trading to select retail clients

items.title: Bitcoin back above $81,000 after hot CPI print, BNB, DOGE lead majors gains
items.desc: Bitcoin rolled back to $81,200 after dipping to $79,800 on Tuesday's hotter-than-expected U.S. inflation reading, while BNB zoomed 2.5% over 24 hours and dogecoin added 1.3% as crypto funds saw their strongest weekly inflows in months.

items.title: Ethereum community launches security feature to end blind signing
items.desc: Crypto platforms Ledger, Trezor, MetaMask, Keycard, WalletConnect, Argot and Fireblocks are among the earliest adopters and contributors to Clear Signing, aimed at ending “blind signing.”

items.title: Kelp DAO eyes reopening withdrawals after rsETH burn
items.desc: Kelp DAO has burned the exploiter’s tokens and outlined a two-week plan to refill rsETH through Aave’s Recovery Guardian multisig wallet.

items.desc: Spokesperson for regulator denies that it put pressure on ARIA Resort & Casino for hosting Predict 2026

items.title: Kelp DAO, Aave to resume rsETH operations as recovery from $292 million exploit progresses
items.desc: Kelp was exploited on April 18 for $292 million from attackers suspected to be part of North Korea's Lazarus Group.

items.title: JPMorgan to launch tokenized money market fund for stablecoin issuers
items.desc: JPMorgan’s filing comes nearly three weeks after rival investment bank Morgan Stanley launched its own money market fund, the Stablecoin Reserves Portfolio.

items.desc: Bermuda Premier David Burt announced several crypto-related initiatives to bring the island nation closer to the industry, including accepting and investing in digital assets and on-chain financial services.

items.desc: Investors buying shares in the instrument gain exposure to the HYPE token without having to own the cryptocurrency.

items.desc: Global banking giant JPMorgan filed for a new tokenized money market fund that will initially run on the Ethereum network.

items.title: JPMorgan files to launch new tokenized fund as Wall Street tokenization race heats up
items.desc: The global banking giant is expanding its tokenized money market lineup, following BlackRock's similar move just a few days ago.

items.title: Senate confirms Kevin Warsh as Federal Reserve governor, with chair vote expected
items.desc: The US Senate voted largely along partisan lines to confirm Kevin Warsh as a member of the Fed's Board of Governors, despite many Democrats’ concerns about the central bank’s independence.

items.title: JPMorgan launching second tokenized money market fund on Ethereum
items.desc: The new fund will invest in U.S. Treasurys and overnight repurchase agreements collateralized by Treasurys or cash.

items.title: Bitcoin dominance rebounds above 58% signaling crypto market consolidation phase
items.desc: Dominance peaked near 62%–63% last year before a sustained drawdown through late 2025, bottoming near 54%–55%.

items.title: What’s Really at Stake in the Market Structure Debate: The BRCA
items.desc: Bitcoin Magazine What’s Really at Stake in the Market Structure Debate: The BRCA The Blockchain Regulatory Certainty Act (BRCA) is the most important piece of the upcoming CLARITY Act. Without BRCA, Clarity leaves developers open to attack. This post What’s Really at Stake in the Market Structure Debate: The BRCA first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Kyle Olney.

items.title: Bitcoin Clings To $80K As Altcoins Drag Market Lower
items.desc: Solana, Cardano and Hyperliquid led the day's losses as risk appetite cooled across digital assets.

items.title: Square Crosses 1 Million Bitcoin-Enabled Merchants as Real-World Adoption Continues to Grow

This post Square Crosses 1 Million Bitcoin-Enabled Merchants as Real-World Adoption Continues to Grow first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.

items.title: Ethereum Foundation rolls out support for Clear Signing crypto security solution
items.desc: Clear Signing is a crypto security feature that aims to simplify transaction descriptions so humans can understand what they're approving.

items.title: The Ethereum Foundation unveils new 'Clear Signing' standard to stop users from approving malicious crypto transactions
items.desc: The move comes after billions in losses from phishing attacks and wallet drains, with Ethereum pushing to make transaction approvals safer and easier to understand.

items.title: Bitcoin Fog appeal tests DOJ theory that global crypto services fall under US law
items.desc: The panel also scrutinized the reliability of the "IP overlap" analysis used to link Sterlingov to Bitcoin Fog.

items.title: Ethereum Foundation Launches Clear Signing Standard
items.desc: The initiative, anchored by ERC-7730 and a new attestation framework, aims to make human-readable transactions the default across wallets and protocols.

items.title: Stablecoin yield infrastructure project raises $13.5M in round led by Sky Ecosystem
items.desc: Osero is coming with three products: Earn, App, and Foundry.

items.title: Privacy emerges as crypto’s next 'killer app,' with Arc, Canton and Tempo topping $1 billion in funding
items.desc: New fundraising rounds for three institution-focused blockchains show how regulation, privacy and corporate competition are reshaping crypto infrastructure, according to Bitwise CIO Matt Hougan.

items.title: DTCC Picks Chainlink As Data Layer For 24/7 Tokenized Collateral Platform
items.desc: The Collateral AppChain will use the Chainlink Runtime Environment to automate eligibility, margining and settlement across global markets, with production launch slated for Q4 2026.

items.title: Coinbase adds Solana-backed loans through Morpho, letting users borrow up to $100,000
items.desc: Coinbase users can now borrow up to $100,000 against their SOL holdings through the exchange's Morpho integration on Base.

items.title: Franklin Templeton, Kraken Parent Payward Team Up to Tokenize Wall Street Products
items.desc: The collaboration between Kraken and Franklin Templeton signals traditional finance's deepening embrace of crypto infrastructure.

items.title: U.S. CFTC in talks with every major pro sports league on policing prediction markets
items.desc: Chairman Michael Selig says the agency has already taken several states to court to prove that sports contracts aren't just betting but that they're derivatives.

items.title: Bitcoin’s bull-bear cycle indicator turns green for first time since March 2023
items.desc: While some experts point to $90,000 as the next explosive level, others caution that this new "early bull" signal isn't a guaranteed crystal ball.

items.title: Binance's chief marketing officer Rachel Conlan to leave the exchange
items.desc: Eowyn Chen, former CEO of Trust Wallet, will serve as her interim replacement.

items.desc: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, NFTs, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: Labor Unions Join Banking Industry in Opposition to Senate Crypto Bill, The Clarity Act
items.desc: Bitcoin Magazine Labor Unions Join Banking Industry in Opposition to Senate Crypto Bill, The Clarity Act Labor unions and major banks are now aligned against the Senate’s CLARITY Act, warning the crypto market structure bill could put retirement savings, bank deposits, and financial stability at risk ahead of Thursday’s pivotal committee vote. This post Labor Unions Join Banking Industry in Opposition to Senate Crypto Bill, The Clarity Act first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.

items.title: Bhutan Moves $8.1M in Bitcoin as Sell-Off Continues
items.desc: The kingdom of Bhutan has offloaded over $230 million in Bitcoin this year while steadily reducing its holdings, according to Arkham data.

items.title: EBay Rejects GameStop's $55 Billion Offer as 'Neither Credible Nor Attractive'
items.desc: Publicly traded online marketplace eBay slammed GameStop's unsolicited $55.5 billion acquisition offer as insufficient.

items.title: Bitcoin digests highest US CPI since 2023 as Fed rate hike woes return
items.desc: Bitcoin showed fresh signs of volatility as US CPI inflation saw new multiyear highs on oil price hikes thanks to the US-Iran war.

items.title: LMAX Group launches digital asset collateral solution for institutions
items.desc: The new portal lets LMAX clients deposit digital assets into custody and use them as collateral to trade FX, metals, CFDs, perpetual futures and crypto.

items.title: Senate Banking Committee Unveils 309-Page Crypto Market Structure Bill Before Thursday Markup
items.desc: Bitcoin Magazine Senate Banking Committee Unveils 309-Page Crypto Market Structure Bill Before Thursday Markup The Senate Banking Committee released the full 309-page text of the Digital Asset Market Clarity Act ahead of Thursday’s markup, setting up a high-stakes battle over stablecoin yield rules. This post Senate Banking Committee Unveils 309-Page Crypto Market Structure Bill Before Thursday Markup first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.

items.title: Anthropic Flags Unauthorized Tokenized Shares
items.desc: The developer of Claude has updated its terms of service, warning against the third-party sale or transfer of its private equity.

items.title: Spot XRP ETFs attract biggest inflows since January
items.desc: The five U.S.-listed spot XRP funds pulled in $25.8 million on Monday, the largest single-day net inflow since Jan. 5, even as ether spot ETFs shed nearly $17 million.

items.title: Exodus sells over 1,000 Bitcoin as Q1 loss widens to $32M
items.desc: Exodus Movement reported a $32.1 million net loss in Q1, with revenue down 36.8% to $22.7 million amid a drop in monthly active users.

items.title: FalconX expands tokenized credit facility to Monad network in lending push
items.desc: FalconX’s tokenized credit vaults can now be used as collateral in DeFi markets on Monad, expanding institutional lending products across blockchain networks.

items.title: North Korea ‘industrialized’ crypto theft, laundered billions: CertiK
items.desc: North Korea-linked hackers stole about $2.06 billion of the $3.4 billion lost in crypto hacks in 2025 and are moving from phishing to physical infiltration, CertiK’s new report finds.

items.title: France’s central banker Beau clashes with Lagarde over private digital euro plans
items.desc: France’s central bank deputy governor told CoinDesk public and private European entities need to get involved in digital asset development, diverging sharply from Christine Lagarde’s stance.

items.title: EBay rejects GameStop’s $56 billion bid, putting bitcoin exposure back in focus
items.desc: The online marketplace's board called the half-cash, half-stock offer "neither credible nor attractive" Tuesday.

items.title: Kraken parent, Franklin Templeton to develop onchain investment products
items.desc: The partnership will focus on tokenized yield products, blockchain-based funds and institutional crypto markets.

items.title: BlackRock Files for New Tokenized Fund With SEC, Taps Securitize Again
items.desc: BlackRock has filed for a new tokenized fund structure with the SEC, selecting Securitize infrastructure for the second time after BUIDL's $2.3B success.

items.title: North Korean Crypto Hackers Stole $2.1B in 2025, 60% of All Losses: CertiK
items.desc: New analysis reveals state-sponsored groups now dominate crypto crime, laundering billions through sophisticated cross-chain networks.

items.title: Morning Minute: Circle Stock Soars After Q1 Beat, $222M Arc Raise
items.desc: Circle just beat earnings and raised fresh capital for its new Arc blockchain, while Michael Saylor explained how and why he'd sell Bitcoin.

items.desc: The draft text contained provisions that could permanently exempt Bitcoin and Ethereum from securities law.

items.title: Is This Bitcoin Bear Market Different? Analysts Weigh In
items.desc: Bitcoin is down 35% from its all-time high, making this drawdown shallower than past cycles, but analysts warn the bear market could resume.

items.title: Three Indicted Over ‘Brazen’ Crypto Wrench Attack Spree in California
items.desc: Federal prosecutors charged the trio with a multi-million dollar crypto theft scheme targeting victims across California.

items.title: Kraken Launches Flexline, Crypto-Backed Lending Product for Builders and Traders
items.desc: Kraken introduced Flexline, a lending product that accepts cryptocurrency as collateral at 10–25% APR fixed rates, targeting crypto-native businesses and high-net-worth individuals excluded from traditional banking.

items.title: Senate Banking Committee Releases 309-Page Clarity Act Draft: US Senate Banking Committee
items.desc: The Senate Banking Committee publicly released the full text of its crypto market structure bill ahead of Thursday's markup, with amendments due by end of business Wednesday.

items.title: Court Greenlights Arbitrum DAO Vote to Move $71M in Recovered Kelp ETH to Aave
items.desc: The court order allows the on-chain Constitutional AIP vote and transfer to proceed without violating the restraining notice, but the freeze itself extends to Aave LLC.

items.title: Clarity Act Vote Set for Thursday: Here's Where the Crypto Bill Stands
items.desc: Crypto leaders are the most confident they’ve been in months—but a major vote on the Senate Banking Committee this week could still go many ways.

items.desc: Circle shared a recap of its Q1 2026 earnings call led by Co-Founder, Chairman and CEO Jeremy Allaire.

items.desc: NEAR Intents upgraded its frontend to enable single-flow swaps from over 100 tokens directly into ZEC, leveraging intent-based architecture for crosschain transactions.

items.title: xStocks Assets Surge Past $100M on Ethereum, $30M on BNB Chain: xStocks
items.desc: Tokenized stock protocol xStocks has crossed $100M market cap on Ethereum with 1,000% YTD growth, while expanding to BNB Chain with $30M in commodities-linked assets.

items.title: Bitcoin Tests $82K As Crypto Funds Notch Sixth Straight Week Of Inflows
items.desc: Crypto investment products absorbed $858 million last week, ahead of the upcoming CLARITY Act markup and Fed chair transition.

items.title: Strategy Resumes Weekly Buys with Smallest BTC Purchase Since December
items.desc: Meanwhile, the largest Ethereum DAT, Bitmine, made its smallest ETH purchase since January, announcing it will slow its weekly purchase pace.

items.desc: Solana's Alpenglow consensus protocol upgrade is one step closer to being rolled out on the layer-1 network's mainnet.

items.title: Ripple Prime Secures $200M Debt Facility to Expand Lending Capacity
items.desc: Funds managed by Neuberger Specialty Finance committed the facility to grow margin financing for the multi-asset prime broker.

items.title: Stream Finance Breaks Six Month Silence With Wind-Down Plan
items.desc: A newly formed Delaware entity will consolidate and liquidate remaining assets, with "strategic alternatives" coming in the next few weeks.

items.title: CVE-2024-52911 - Script Interpreter Remote Crash
items.desc: After Bitcoin Core 0.14.0 and before Bitcoin Core 29.0, validating a specially-crafted block may cause the node to access previously freed memory. During validation, necessary data required for checking inputs for each transaction is pre-calculated and cached. For specially crafted invalid blocks, it was possible for this data to be destroyed while it was still being accessed by a background validation thread. An attacker capable of mining a block with sufficient proof-of-work could have exploited this to crash victim nodes. Because of the nature of use-after-free bugs, it is possible that the crash could have been used for remote code execution, though constraints on the input (block) data make this unlikely. This issue is considered High severity. Details By default, script validation for new blocks is dispatched to background threads via a vector of CScriptCheck functors. Each CScriptCheck holds a pointer to a PrecomputedTransactionData object which stores some data needed by each input in the transaction. Because it stores a pointer and not the data itself, care must be taken to ensure that the PrecomputedTransactionData outlives the CScriptCheck. The script checks lifetime is enforced by an RAII class, CCheckQueueControl. However, the control is intantiated before the precomputed transaction data. Because local objects in C++ are destructed in reverse order of construction, this means the vector of PrecomputedTransactionData is destroyed before the CCheckQueueControl. This is not an issue when the block is valid, as CCheckQueueControl::Wait() will be called before the function returns and the PrecomputedTransactionData gets destroyed. However, in case of an early return (when a separate check fails) a background script thread may read the precomputed transaction data after it was destroyed. An attacker could exploit this to crash victim nodes at the expense of a valid PoW at tip. Attribution Cory Fields (MIT DCI) discovered this vulnerability and responsibly disclosed it in a detailed report containing a proof of concept for reproduction and a proposed mitigation. Timeline 2024-11-02 Cory Fields privately reports the bug 2024-11-06 Pieter Wuille pushes a covert fix to already open PR #31112 which works around the issue by removing the early returns 2024-12-03 PR #31112 is merged 2025-04-12 Bitcoin Core version 29.0 is released with a fix 2026-04-19 The last vulnerable Bitcoin Core version (28.x) goes end of life 2026-05-05 Public disclosure.

items.title: Bitcoin Core 31.0 released
